<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./supabase/functions/send-waitlist-confirmation/index.ts`

```text
1	// Supabase Edge Function: send a plain-text waitlist confirmation email.
2	//
3	// Triggered by a Supabase Database Webhook on INSERT into public.waitlist.
4	// The webhook POSTs the new row; we authenticate the call with a shared secret
5	// header (the function URL is public) and send the email via the Resend API.
6	//
7	// Required secrets (set with `supabase secrets set`):
8	//   RESEND_API_KEY            - Resend API key with sending permission
9	//   WAITLIST_WEBHOOK_SECRET   - shared secret, also sent by the webhook as the
10	//                               `x-webhook-secret` header
11	//
12	// Deploy with:  supabase functions deploy send-waitlist-confirmation --no-verify-jwt
13	
14	interface WaitlistRecord {
15	  first_name?: string;
16	  last_name?: string;
17	  email?: string;
18	}
19	
20	interface WebhookPayload {
21	  type?: string;
22	  table?: string;
23	  record?: WaitlistRecord;
24	}
25	
26	const FROM = "Atlia <founders@atlia.com>";
27	const REPLY_TO = "founders@atlia.com";
28	
29	function confirmationText(firstName: string): string {
30	  const name = firstName ? ` ${firstName}` : "";
31	  return `Hi${name},
32	
33	Thanks for joining the list — we'll be in contact soon.
34	
35	If you have any questions, feel free to text us at (909) 729-6282 or (732) 610-3038.
36	
37	Shaan and Edmond`;
38	}
39	
40	Deno.serve(async (req) => {
41	  if (req.method !== "POST") {
42	    return new Response("Method not allowed", { status: 405 });
43	  }
44	
45	  // Authenticate the webhook call against our shared secret.
46	  const expectedSecret = Deno.env.get("WAITLIST_WEBHOOK_SECRET");
47	  const providedSecret = req.headers.get("x-webhook-secret");
48	  if (!expectedSecret || providedSecret !== expectedSecret) {
49	    return new Response("Unauthorized", { status: 401 });
50	  }
51	
52	  const resendApiKey = Deno.env.get("RESEND_API_KEY");
53	  if (!resendApiKey) {
54	    console.error("RESEND_API_KEY is not set");
55	    return new Response("Server not configured", { status: 500 });
56	  }
57	
58	  let payload: WebhookPayload;
59	  try {
60	    payload = await req.json();
61	  } catch {
62	    return new Response("Invalid JSON", { status: 400 });
63	  }
64	
65	  const record = payload.record;
66	  const email = record?.email?.trim();
67	  if (!email) {
68	    console.error("No email in webhook payload", payload);
69	    return new Response("No email to send to", { status: 400 });
70	  }
71	
72	  const resendResp = await fetch("https://api.resend.com/emails", {
73	    method: "POST",
74	    headers: {
75	      Authorization: `Bearer ${resendApiKey}`,
76	      "Content-Type": "application/json",
77	    },
78	    body: JSON.stringify({
79	      from: FROM,
80	      reply_to: REPLY_TO,
81	      to: email,
82	      subject: "You're on the Atlia waitlist",
83	      text: confirmationText(record?.first_name?.trim() ?? ""),
84	    }),
85	  });
86	
87	  if (!resendResp.ok) {
88	    const detail = await resendResp.text();
89	    console.error(`Resend error ${resendResp.status}: ${detail}`);
90	    // Non-2xx so the failure is visible in webhook/function logs. The signup is
91	    // already committed, so this does not affect the user joining the waitlist.
92	    return new Response("Failed to send email", { status: 502 });
93	  }
94	
95	  return new Response(JSON.stringify({ ok: true }), {
96	    status: 200,
97	    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
98	  });
99	});
100
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-01 17:35:55Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-01 17:35:57Z)**_

The mobile issue is clear: the paired fields (First/Last name, Phone/Referral, City/State) only stack below 360px, so on typical phones (375–430px) they're cramped side-by-side with long placeholders getting clipped. Also inputs are 15.2px, which triggers iOS auto-zoom on focus. Let me improve the `640px` mobile block and remove the too-narrow 360px rule.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-01 17:36:03Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Edit"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Edit**</summary>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-01 17:36:10Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Typecheck and build

`npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20; echo "---build---"; npm run build 2>&1 | tail -15`

> tsc -b && vite build

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-01 17:36:17Z)**_

Build passes. Here's what I changed to make the Sign up form look better on mobile (all in the `@media (max-width: 640px)` block in `src/App.css`):

**The main fixes**
- **Paired fields now stack** — First/Last name, Phone/Referral, and City/State previously only stacked below 360px, so on real phones (375–430px) they were squeezed side-by-side and long placeholders like "Referral name or code." got clipped. They now go full-width, giving each field comfortable room.
- **No more iOS zoom-on-focus** — inputs were 15.2px, which makes Safari auto-zoom when you tap a field. Bumped to 16px on mobile so the viewport stays put.

**Polish**
- Turned the centered dialog into a **bottom sheet** on phones — full-width, rounded top corners only, anchored to the bottom edge with a subtle upward slide-in animation. This is the expected mobile pattern and gives more usable height.
- Respected the **safe-area inset** at the bottom so the submit button clears the home indicator on notched iPhones.
- Slightly larger submit tap target and tightened the header spacing so the "Sign up" title clears the close button.

The desktop layout (centered 540px modal) is untouched. Want me to spin up the dev server so you can check it in a mobile viewport?
